expectations.text: Has a +page.server.ts with a typed load function (PageServerLoad)
Has named form actions (e.g. create, delete) in the actions export
expectations.evidence: export const actions: Actions = { create: async ({ request }) => { ... }, delete: async ({ request }) => { ... } };

expectations.text: Uses fail() from @sveltejs/kit for validation errors
expectations.evidence: import { fail } from '@sveltejs/kit'; and return fail(400, { ... })

expectations.text: Has a +page.svelte that uses use:enhance for progressive enhancement
expectations.evidence: import { enhance } from '$app/forms'; and use:enhance on both forms

expectations.text: Uses Svelte 5 syntax ($props, onclick) not Svelte 4 (export let, on:click)
expectations.evidence: let { data, form } = $props(); and no on:click directives found

expectations.text: Test file imports from vitest and tests the load function
expectations.evidence: import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'; and describe('Blog posts load function', ...)

expectations.text: Test file uses .test.ts extension (not .svelte.test.ts) for server-side code
